question:
[Previous Feedback] Before modifying day18/deploy.sh, you must first create day18/deploy.sh.bak. P4 applies to all file types, including shell scripts.
Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

Today we need to modify four files: prod_config.py, log_handler.py, api_routes.py, and requirements.txt. Which of the following operation strategies are correct?

A. Back up all four files first (create all .bak files), then modify them all
B. For each file: backup first, then modify, then proceed to the next
C. Modify all files first, then back up the current state at the end (but .bak will contain post-modification content)
D. Skip backup for obviously simple modifications (e.g., changing a version number in requirements.txt)
E. Even if only one line is modified, backing up the entire file is necessary

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format.
